methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Magdrive is a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Mark Stokes and Thomas Clayson that has raised $22.5M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though narrow-stage peer medians lack sufficient depth for reliable anchoring.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method given data constraints. Using the $22.5M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage transitions yields an implied valuation of $56M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied without disclosed revenue; the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S of 49.8x across the five public tickers cannot be meaningfully applied to a private, pre-revenue or early-revenue company. Peer-median triangulation is similarly inapplicable—while 23 same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-tier) returned fewer than three datapoints, making sectoral rollup unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $48M to $65M, with a central estimate of $56M. Key uncertainties include confirmation of development stage and near-term revenue trajectory; the single-method basis means any adjustment to stage-progression assumptions (multiplier variance, revenue inflection timing) materially affects the range. Public comparables suggest the space sector commands high multiples, but without Magdrive's revenue visibility, anchoring to those multiples remains premature.
